title: Jonah Study method definition — clustering constant near 0.39

cycle_definition.cycle_start: selection of a dataset where subjects have little or no agency over the recorded outcome
cycle_definition.cycle_end: full measurable cycle for the phenomenon is defined and bounded

irreversible_event.event_definition: the point in the measured cycle at which irreversible change clusters most densely

linked_files: Jonah Study — Patent Method Replication Guide.pdf

notes.method_note: Method defines a measurable constant near 0.39 in datasets where subjects have little or no control over the outcome, using cycle length as denominator and peak irreversible clustering position as numerator.
notes.confidence_note: Conceptual anchor record drawn from method definition rather than a single empirical dataset instance.
notes.review_note: Use later research records to map concrete dataset examples such as male-pattern baldness, shelter euthanasia timing, or migration departure timing.
